one_time_description: Agricultural Benefits Settlement expense of $1,532,918 (prior year Ammunition and Twine Settlement revenue of $2,192,724)
economic_notes: Horse Lake First Nation owns 100% of shares in multiple entities including Horse Lake First Nation Investment Management Board, 424337 Alberta Ltd., Horse Lake Oil & Gas Corporation, Horse Lake Industry Relations Corporation, and HLFN Oilfield Construction Corp. Agricultural Benefits Settlement Trust holds $30.1M in portfolio investments. Federal Trust Funds total $1,836,234.
notes: Audit is qualified on two bases: (1) investments in related First Nation entities use modified equity method rather than full consolidation, with financial records unavailable for 4 of 5 entities; (2) asset retirement obligations not estimated or recorded. Accumulated surplus per statement of financial position is $69,468,252 but statement of operations shows $73,114,825 — difference relates to remeasurement losses of ($3,646,573). Agricultural Benefits Settlement Trust fully consolidated with $30.1M in portfolio investments and significant unrealized losses of ($4,246,297). Prior year had Ammunition and Twine Settlement of $2,192,724 as one-time revenue. RBC loan of $5,610,474 matures November 2023 creating significant current portion of long-term debt ($6,269,098 due in 2024).
